title: Five things the World Cup taught marketers about creators
tldr: A concrete post-mortem of how creator marketing performed under the pressure of the 2026 World Cup, with tactical takeaways. Timely campaign breakdown tied to a huge cultural moment.
why_fresh: fresh campaign takeaways from a just-concluded mega event
angle: What the World Cup revealed about the limits and strengths of creator-led brand storytelling at scale.

title: The 2026 World Cup's First Winner: the Creator Economy
tldr: Argues that creators, not broadcasters, were the biggest beneficiaries of the World Cup's attention economy. Reinforces a shift in how audiences consume live events.
why_fresh: new angle on media-consumption shift during live sports
angle: How live-event audiences now flow to creators over traditional broadcast — and what that means for owned media.

title: 'It's a CEO conversation,' Accenture and Whalar on their playbook for the creator economy
tldr: Accenture and Whalar reframe creator marketing as a CEO-level strategic issue, not just a CMO channel play. Signals the mainstreaming of the creator economy into corporate strategy.
why_fresh: repositioning of creator economy as C-suite topic
angle: Why the creator economy is becoming a board-level narrative and how comms leaders should frame it.

title: Internal communications in 2026: A mid-year review
tldr: A mid-year audit of what's actually shaping internal comms in 2026 — AI integration, tooling, and shifting priorities. Useful pulse-check for comms strategists.
angle: What the first half of 2026 tells us about where internal comms and employee engagement are really heading.

title: Why "AI Productivity Gains" Is the Wrong Pitch for Every Stakeholder Above You
tldr: Argues content leaders should reframe AI's value per stakeholder — CFO, CMO, legal — rather than selling generic productivity. Practical for defending budget and headcount.
why_fresh: fresh framing on stakeholder communication of AI value
angle: How to translate AI's content impact into the language each executive stakeholder actually cares about.

title: AI-Ready or AI-Exposed? Rethinking the Role of SharePoint in Your Digital Workplace
tldr: Warns that AI will surface every governance gap and inconsistency in your content estate at scale. Timely case for content governance discipline.
why_fresh: new governance angle on AI readiness
angle: Why AI turns sloppy content governance into a visible liability — and how to get your house in order first.

title: B2B influencer marketing hits 85% adoption as brands shift budget toward industry experts
tldr: Reports that B2B influencer marketing has hit 85% adoption as budgets move to industry experts over generic influencers. A concrete data point on the human-expert-over-polish trend.
why_fresh: new adoption data on expert-led B2B content
angle: The rise of the employee-expert as brand voice: why B2B is betting on people over polish.

title: Sociable: YouTube highlights brand partnerships with sports creators
tldr: YouTube's latest report examines the rising impact of athletic influencers and how brands can connect with them. Data-backed look at a fast-growing creator niche.
angle: How sports creators are becoming a distinct content category brands must plan for.

title: Netflix eyes Letterboxd in push to lift engagement
tldr: Netflix is reportedly targeting Letterboxd to boost engagement, signaling how community-driven platforms shape media-consumption habits. A telling move about owning audience communities.
angle: Why streamers are chasing niche community platforms to own audience engagement, not just content.

title: How Bayer's head of media is using AI-powered predictive targeting and navigating AI search
tldr: A brand-side view on deploying AI predictive targeting and adapting to AI-driven search. Concrete practitioner perspective on AI's impact on media planning.
angle: How brands are re-tooling content for AI search and predictive targeting — a comms playbook.

title: Sadoun says 'clients don't want partners that are cutting jobs' as Publicis lifts forecast
tldr: Publicis CEO argues clients favor agencies that invest in people over those cutting jobs, as it raises its outlook amid rivals' layoffs. A sharp reputation and positioning contrast in the holding-company world.
angle: People-first positioning as competitive advantage: reading the Publicis-vs-WPP reputation contrast.

title: Why MLS's largest-ever coordinated marketing campaign isn't really about Lionel Messi
tldr: A breakdown of MLS's biggest coordinated campaign and why the strategy transcends its star. Useful campaign case study on narrative beyond a single figurehead.
angle: Building a durable brand narrative that doesn't depend on a single celebrity face.

title: The end of ownership is the end of mass media's promise
tldr: Uses PlayStation's digital shift to argue that the erosion of ownership undermines a core promise of mass media. A provocative essay on media models and audience trust.
why_fresh: fresh conceptual take on media ownership
angle: What the death of ownership means for how audiences relate to and trust the content they 'buy'.

title: 4 ways challenger brands can outsmart industry leaders
tldr: Practical tactics for smaller brands to win against larger, better-funded competitors through big ideas over big budgets. Actionable for storytelling and positioning.
angle: How challenger-brand narrative strategy beats budget — with a modern content twist.

title: How to build a cross-network brand monitoring strategy on social
tldr: Argues social has replaced the traditional news cycle and lays out a cross-network brand monitoring approach. Ties directly to media monitoring and share-of-voice work.
angle: Building a real-time listening system now that social is the news cycle.
